agent_raw: The retrospective results do not support mobile-van deployment. Three issues are present: (1) verification bias — specialist labels were assigned only to patients who completed exams after referral, creating a biased label set that may overestimate the model's sensitivity in an unselected mobile screening population; (2) device and operator shift — lower-cost cameras and non-specialist nurses create an untested distribution shift from the hospital-center training data; (3) referral capacity tradeoff uncharacterized — the aggregate hospital metrics do not show what sensitivity and referral volume occur at the operating threshold in the lower-prevalence mobile population. The QC rule for unreadable images is a sensible safeguard, not a hidden flaw. Lower prevalence changes threshold calibration needs but is not alone a disqualifier. Prospective validation on mobile images with independent adjudication of all sampled patients is required before deployment decisions.
